headline: Agentic coding workflows and self-improving loops led the week as Claude Fable's discontinuation reshaped the model field.
body: Agent-first tooling and recursive self-improvement topped the rotation, with autonomous loops like Codex's /goal Ralph that plan, execute, and test code without user intervention, and self-improving knowledge bases built on Obsidian, Claude, and NotebookLM. RAG, test-time behavior, and project management entered as new techniques, while OpenRouter's Fusion API drew scrutiny: testing showed its judge-routed ensemble underperforms single strong models on coding, math, and SVG tasks despite Fable-level marketing claims. AI Search confirmed Claude Fable's discontinuation alongside open-source releases including GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, and MiniMax M3. On the front-end side, prop drilling surfaced as a new entrant via react-call, which calls React components as asynchronous functions to cut state boilerplate.
